page: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at 2.0 GHz with 4 physical cores and a single thread per core under a shared allocation model. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and lacks dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates highly capable single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics and single-threaded bzip3 decompression both ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded gzip compression and large-block memory write bandwidth are also strong, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-threaded performance is more moderate; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations fall within the average range. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput are weaker, landing in the bottom 25%, while medium-model LLM prompt processing registers in the bottom 10%.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that benefit from modern AMD Genoa instruction sets (such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography) without requiring dedicated CPU cores. The primary tradeoff lies in the shared CPU allocation, which can introduce performance variability under sustained multi-threaded loads, as reflected in the lower multi-threaded benchmark tiers compared to its leading single-core results.

This server is well-suited for general-purpose applications, single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight caching layers, and development environments. It is also appropriate for basic web serving and small-scale database operations that do not demand sustained, non-shared multi-core execution.
description: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 16.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities and strong single-threaded decompression speeds, though multi-threaded operations and database performance fall into average or weak categories due to the shared CPU allocation. The instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AES. It represents a qualitatively cost-effective option for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded tasks, and development environments that require modern CPU features but can tolerate shared resource allocation.

tagline: A shared-core AMD Genoa instance offering strong single-threaded performance and balanced memory-to-vCPU resources for general-purpose workloads.

16.0 GB RAM and 80 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Supports AVX-512, vector, and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Best suited for general-purpose, single-threaded, and development workloads
